summary: My family originally came from Laos before we fled to Thailand in 1979 during the Vietnam War. Although I was born in Thailand, our roots are in Laos, and it was the conflict and instability there that forced us to leave. Laos represents the beginning of my family's journey as refugees seeking safety and a better life.

I was born in a refugee camp in Thailand after my family fled Laos in 1979 during the Vietnam War. We stayed in the camp for some time, but the Thai government eventually didn't want the Hmong people to remain there. This pushed us to immigrate to the United States with the help of the International Organization for Migration.

The Vietnam War was the backdrop to my family's displacement from Laos to Thailand in 1979. It was the conflict that uprooted us and led to our life in a refugee camp and eventually to immigrate to the United States. The war's impact shaped much of my early life and my family's struggle for safety.

We came to the United States through a program called the International Organization for Migration, or IOM. They helped us find a sponsor who supported us when we arrived at the Wisconsin airport in 2004. My sponsor was a great help for eight years, assisting with paperwork, welfare, and daily needs like food and laundry.

We landed at the Wisconsin airport on November 1, 2004, where my sponsor took a picture of my family. I only stayed in Wisconsin for three days before moving to Minnesota to live with my uncle. Wisconsin was the first place I set foot in America, and that moment is captured in a photo full of memories.

After arriving in the U.S., I moved to Minnesota to live with my uncle Lee, who thought Saint Paul had better schools and more job opportunities. My first impression of Minnesota was seeing pumpkins everywhere, which I later learned was part of Halloween celebrations. Minnesota became my new home where I raised my son and adjusted to life in America.

When I first arrived in Minnesota, I noticed pumpkins at almost every house, which I later understood was how people celebrated Halloween. It was a new cultural experience for me as I adapted to life in America. Halloween stands out as one of my earliest memories of Minnesota and the American way of life.
